seo.title: Product Data API - Fresh GTIN-first product price data
seo.description: Developer-ready product price data by GTIN with freshness metadata, provenance, and no-data-no-pay billing.

hero.title: Fresh product price data, metered only when usable data is returned.
hero.subtitle: Query a product by GTIN and receive sanitized offers, best prices, freshness metadata, provenance labels, and credit metering in one REST response.

fresh.title: Freshness
fresh.caption: Fresh offer window for billable price data.

nodata.title: Billing
nodata.caption: Invalid GTINs, missing products, and stale offers cost zero credits.

gtin.title: Lookup
gtin.caption: Strict checksum validation before any credit reservation.

provenance.title: Offers
provenance.caption: Merchant labels without internal datasource leakage.

api.title: One call to the price facet
api.subtitle: The response envelope includes data and metering metadata for clients that need deterministic billing behavior.

billing.title: Billing rules
billing.subtitle: Credits are consumed only when the product price facet returns usable fresh data.
rules: Product not found: 404 response, zero credits.
Empty or stale price data: 200 response, zero credits.
Fresh offer data: 200 response, billable credit debit.

seo.title: Pricing - Product Data API
seo.description: Credit packs and subscriptions for Product Data API, loaded from the backend billing catalog.

pricing.title: Self-serve pricing
pricing.subtitle: Buy credits as packs or subscribe for monthly credits with rollover. Pricing is loaded from the live backend catalog.
pricing.errorDescription: The billing catalog could not be loaded right now.
pricing.packDescription: One-time credits for production API calls.
pricing.subscriptionDescription: Monthly credits with a {months}-month rollover cap.

seo.title: FAQ - Product Data API
seo.description: Answers about Product Data API coverage, freshness, billing, and support.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Short answers for developers and buyers evaluating the price API.

items.question: When are credits consumed?
items.answer: Credits are consumed only when the requested facet returns usable fresh data.

items.question: What happens when no product is found?
items.answer: The API returns a non-billable error response. Missing products do not consume credits.

items.question: Is stock or shipping data included?
items.answer: No. v1 focuses on sanitized price offers, freshness, and provenance.

seo.title: Contact - Product Data API
seo.description: Contact support or sales for Product Data API.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Reach the team for support, billing questions, or enterprise evaluation.
contact.supportCopy: For account, API, or billing issues, include your organization name and request ID when available.
contact.salesCopy: For larger volumes or procurement questions, contact sales with your expected monthly call volume.

seo.title: Legal - Product Data API
seo.description: Legal documents for Product Data API.

legal.title: Legal
legal.subtitle: Privacy and terms documents for the Product Data API service.

seo.title: Privacy - Product Data API
seo.description: Privacy information for Product Data API.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.subtitle: How Product Data API handles account, billing, and usage data.
paragraphs: Product Data API stores account, organization, authentication, billing, and API usage metadata needed to operate the service.
API keys are stored hashed. Clear API secrets are shown only when created or rotated.
Usage records are retained for billing, support, abuse prevention, and service reliability.

seo.title: Terms - Product Data API
seo.description: Terms of service for Product Data API.

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.subtitle: Core service terms for Product Data API customers.
paragraphs: Product Data API is provided as a metered product data service. Customers are responsible for protecting their API keys.
Credits are billed according to the no-data-no-pay rules documented for each facet.
The v1 service exposes product price data only. Future facets may be added under their own contracts.

dashboard.title: Product Data API
dashboard.description: Developer portal for the metered B2B product data API.

benchmarks.title: Inference energy analytics
benchmarks.subtitle: Neutral, request-level energy attribution foundations for internal analytics and future reporting mappings.
benchmarks.notice: The previous public benchmark index is retired. Operator analytics now use auditable request-level Joule events with quality and confidence metadata.

metrics.unit_caption: Each inference event is stored in Joules before any derived KPI.
metrics.derived_caption: Dashboards derive J/token and Wh/1k tokens from neutral event data.
metrics.quality_caption: Method tier, uncertainty, and exclusions stay attached to every event.

table.event_level_caption: The agent samples power during live inference windows and emits one auditable event per request.
table.neutral_caption: The platform stores Joules, tokens, measurement method, confidence, and exclusions without embedding compliance claims.
table.compatibility_caption: Additional reporting mappings remain possible later by transforming retained evidence instead of rewriting the raw ledger.

page.title: Energy consumption
page.subtitle: Request-level energy consumed by your API usage, grouped by model, API key, node, and runtime slot.

admin.title: Energy accountability board
admin.subtitle: Consolidated request-level energy evidence across clients, nodes, API keys, and measurement methods.

energy.load_error: Energy analytics could not be loaded.

chart.title: Energy over time
chart.no_data: No energy events in this period.

seo.title: Infera - Sovereign decentralized AI cloud
seo.description: Sovereign, decentralized, cost-efficient and ecological AI cloud, OpenAI-compatible.

hero.subtitle: Connect underused GPUs or route AI workloads through an OpenAI-compatible API designed for resilience and energy efficiency.
hero.surfaceEyebrow: One control plane, two growth paths
hero.surfaceTitle: Shared orchestration for request routing and compute monetization
hero.graphLabel: Network graph with client, router and GPU nodes

compatibility.label: Preserve SDKs and existing request flows.

security.label: Match routing guarantees to workload criticality.

activation.label: Adopt as a client or contribute capacity as a provider.

enterprise.label: For enterprise teams
enterprise.title: Move workloads without rewriting your stack
enterprise.text: Keep OpenAI client compatibility while adding routing, sovereignty and tiered trust controls.

provider.label: For compute providers
provider.title: Activate idle GPUs with policy-aware demand
provider.text: Expose local capacity through a pull-based agent model designed for security and operational control.

metrics.label: Public network statistics driving the orbital animation

index1.seoTitle: Infera orbital network hero - Aurora variant
index1.seoDescription: Aurora orbital landing page variant for the Infera peer-to-peer inference network.
index1.title: A living network for sovereign inference
index1.subtitle: Requests move through a pull-based peer mesh where nearby GPU providers, router policy, and trust tiers stay visible in one premium motion system.

index2.seoTitle: Infera orbital network hero - Particle variant
index2.seoDescription: Full page particle landing variant for the Infera decentralized AI cloud.
index2.title: Inference that feels geographically alive
index2.subtitle: A full-screen cluster field seeded from node activity, token flow, and routed jobs to express Infera as an active peer-to-peer compute fabric.

index3.seoTitle: Infera - The Sovereign Fabric for Trusted AI
index3.seoDescription: Sovereign, decentralized, and eco-friendly AI inference network. Join as a provider or access our enterprise-grade API.
index3.subtitle: A decentralized inference network powered by underused GPUs. Built for sovereignty, verified for security, and designed for maximum energy efficiency.

entryPoints.title: Four ways to start with Infera
entryPoints.subtitle: Reduce AI costs, monetize available compute, federate local capacity or govern your AI APIs: each path keeps market standards while making routing, trust and costs controllable.

consume.title: Reduce AI workload costs
consume.kicker: For product, IT, data and operations teams
consume.description: Progressively move open-weight-friendly workloads to an OpenAI-compatible API without rewriting integrations. Start with fast-ROI flows, then extend policies for cost, residency, availability and trust.

points.1: Keep SDKs, prompts, API keys and quotas during the transition.
points.2: Prioritize RAG, extraction, classification, internal support and document batch workloads.
points.3: Route by cost, latency, availability and Public, Trusted or regulated trust level.

provide.title: Monetize available compute
provide.kicker: For individuals, workstations and internal fleets
provide.description: Let a machine or fleet contribute when it is available, without exposing LocalAI directly and without moving private keys outside the node.

points.1: The agent pulls tasks: there is no inbound router push surface.
points.2: You control availability windows, commitment level and node shutdown.
points.3: Useful capacity can become billable when it matches network demand.

federate.title: Federate local capacity
federate.kicker: For institutions, territories and cloud operators
federate.description: Bring trusted actors into a governed AI capacity layer: service continuity, local pooling, measurable sovereignty and shared rules.

points.1: Organize municipalities, campuses, hospitals, nonprofits, companies or cloud operators by scope.
points.2: Set priorities, trust levels and fallback routes before production.
points.3: Make cost, energy, availability and audit evidence visible enough to govern.

govern.title: Govern your AI APIs
govern.kicker: For IT, security, compliance and finance
govern.description: Centralize AI usage control: routing policies, audit trails, sensitive-data detection, privacy, costs, quotas and trust levels.

points.1: Track who consumes what, through which key, model and trust level.
points.2: Add privacy and audit controls before expanding usage.
points.3: Keep frontier models elsewhere when needed, while steering relevant open-weight routes.

modelRail.title: An offer is credible only when model, hardware and trust level are aligned.
modelRail.subtitle: The catalog helps scope realistic workloads: internal assistants, document search, extraction, classification, code, vision, speech and batch processing.

mapExplainer.title: The Infera network, node by node
mapExplainer.subtitle: Public node markers show live status, hardware, health, model readiness, and GeoIP-derived locations.

decentralized.title: Decentralized by design
decentralized.text: GPU and CPU resources from individuals and businesses distributed across Europe - no central datacenter.

proximity.title: Compute close to data
proximity.text: Smart routing to the most available and geographically closest nodes.

sovereign.title: Sovereign and compliant
sovereign.text: No dependency on US or Asian hyperscalers. Trust-policy-based routing control.

sovereignty.title: Sovereignty
sovereignty.description: Native decentralization and no lock-in to hyperscalers.

compatibility.title: Simple integration
compatibility.description: OpenAI compatibility to preserve existing application flows.

security.title: Tiered security
security.description: Public, Trusted and HDS grids based on criticality.

how.title: How it works
how.subtitle: A production path designed to stay simple for clients and controlled for providers.

client.description: Applications keep a familiar API surface while choosing the right trust envelope for each workload.

points.2: Preserve migration simplicity for product and engineering teams.

router.description: The control plane evaluates routing, node trust, and availability before dispatching work.

points.1: Apply policy-aware placement across public, trusted, or regulated grids.
points.2: Coordinate supply and demand without exposing provider internals.

network.description: Agents pull tasks from the network and execute them close to the hardware boundary.

points.1: Keep private keys on-node and enforce mTLS between services.
points.2: Serve local inference through sidecar-controlled access.

metrics.title: Economical & ecological
metrics.subtitle: Sobriety only matters when it is measurable: existing capacity, workload-aware routing, and exportable evidence for sustainability, procurement, and finance teams.

carbon.title: AI carbon accounting compatible with CSRD Scope 3
carbon.description: Infera connects tokens, models, execution pools, periods, and energy signals to provide usable AI footprint evidence, not just a global estimate.

points.usage: Usage-level measurement to separate experimentation, production, batch, and fallback.
points.scope3: CSRD Scope 3-compatible reading for digital-service procurement and outsourced AI usage.
points.exports: Exports designed to connect consumption, cost, logical location, and data confidence level.

networkMap.title: See node capacity before you route workload
networkMap.subtitle: The network map shows direct node markers with status, hardware, runtime, and model readiness from backend GeoIP-enriched node data.
networkMap.mapSubtitle: Direct node capacity and your approximate region.
networkMap.empty: No public node location is available yet.
networkMap.error: The public node map is temporarily unavailable.

1.a: The router picks the closest available node geographically.

2.a: Public is best effort, Trusted is monitored, HDS is contractual.

3.a: EU, GDPR, HDS and SecNumCloud drive infrastructure policies.

4.a: KYC checks, reputation scoring and operational validation.

5.a: Open-weight model families served via LocalAI by selected tier.

6.a: Yes, key endpoints are preserved to minimize migration overhead.

join.title: Join the network.
join.subtitle: Priority beta access whether you bring compute capacity or reroute AI requests.
join.note: No spam. One email when your access is ready.
join.success: Thanks, your priority request has been recorded.

footer.baseline: Fresh GTIN-first product price data with no-data-no-pay billing.
footer.variants: v0 · v1 dark · v2 soft · v3 editorial

retire.title: Retire node

detail.metadata_empty: No metadata exposed by this node.

card.live_routing_caption: Capabilities published by the node's active heartbeat.
card.no_live_models: No models are loaded in live routing.

card.no_provisioned_models: No provisioned models in the scanner summary.
card.runtime_caption: Identity, runtime and security posture for the node.

seo.description: Sign in to access your Infera backoffice, admin console, and node operations.

login.tagline: The sovereign network for trusted AI inference.
login.providerUnavailable: This provider is not configured yet.

seo.title: Infera - Offers by audience
seo.description: Navigate offers for individuals, enterprises, and institutional programs with a common menu.

hero.title: Infera offers
hero.subtitle: Choose the right engagement model: contribute capacity, industrialize AI workloads, or organize a trusted territorial grid.

particuliers.title: Individuals
particuliers.subtitle: Monetize idle compute and join the grid

entreprises.title: Enterprises
entreprises.subtitle: Sovereign API, governance, and resilient operations

overview.title: Audience-first packaging
overview.description: The same Infera network can be positioned differently depending on expectations, governance, and procurement constraints.

points.1: Keep token billing transparent and auditable.
points.2: Separate base grid usage from fallback guarantees.
points.3: Expose roadmap items (batch mode, P2P) as contract-ready options.

docs.title: Reference documentation
docs.description: Commercial offers, pricing multipliers, and packaging rationale are documented in public docs.

seo.title: Infera - Earn with your PC through sovereign AI
seo.description: Estimate your PC revenue potential, choose your engagement level, and download the Infera agent.

hero.badge: For gamers, creators, and PCs that sleep too much
hero.title: Earn with your PC while helping sovereign AI.
hero.subtitle: Your machine can run AI models for the Infera network when you are not using it. You choose your engagement level, stay in control, and useful capacity can generate revenue.

earn.title: Your GPU can earn
earn.text: Turn idle machine hours into paid inference capacity.

control.title: You stay in control
control.text: Start, pause, or adjust availability around your own usage.

mission.title: Useful, not just profitable
mission.text: Help build a more distributed, local, and sovereign AI alternative.

rig.workload: AI tasks sent when the machine is free

items.idle: Works mainly during your available windows.
items.paid: Revenue follows capacity that is actually useful.

how.title: Your PC becomes a small AI compute node.
how.subtitle: Infera connects AI inference demand with available machines. For you, the experience should stay simple: install, choose when to contribute, and track what it earns.

install.title: Install the agent
install.text: Download the Infera agent, then your machine prepares the required environment without exposing your AI tools directly to the internet.

schedule.title: Choose your windows
schedule.text: Use idle hours, nights, days when the PC stays on, or a more serious commitment if you have a dedicated machine.

compute.title: The network sends work
compute.text: When your node is available, it receives AI tasks matched to its profile. Your machine works for real usage, not a pointless benchmark.

earn.title: Track revenue
earn.text: Revenue depends on your hardware, demand, availability, and the engagement level accepted by the network.

simulator.title: Estimate what your machine can earn.
simulator.subtitle: The simulator detects browser-visible signals, estimates your hardware profile, and compares Public potential with the Trusted uplift.
simulator.note: Aggressive but honest read: this is not guaranteed income. It is a potential projection based on your hardware, availability, and network demand.

tiers.title: The more trust you bring, the more your capacity is worth.
tiers.subtitle: Not everyone wants to dedicate a full machine. Infera separates a flexible personal-PC mode from Trusted mode for contributors ready to go further.

public.title: Public
public.subtitle: The flexible mode for your everyday PC
public.text: Contribute when your machine is available without turning your setup into a permanent server. It is the natural way to start, test revenue potential, and keep your usual comfort.

points.gaming: You can keep gaming, creating, or working alongside it.
points.flexible: You control when the machine participates.

trusted.title: Trusted
trusted.subtitle: The premium mode for more qualified capacity
trusted.text: Trusted targets workloads that require more confidence. In exchange for potentially higher revenue, the machine must boot into a dedicated or controlled environment with less improvisation.

points.premium: Capacity is valued higher when the network needs guarantees.
points.dedicated: Designed for a dedicated machine, dual boot, or controlled operating system.
points.secure: A stricter path to inspire confidence for end customers.

apple.title: Apple Silicon starts with an advantage.
apple.text: Thanks to the hardware characteristics and tight integration of recent Apple machines, Infera presents Apple nodes as Trusted candidates by default. For the user, that means a more premium story: your machine can join the secure tier directly, with better-qualified capacity for the network.

security.title: Your PC contributes. It does not become an open door.
security.subtitle: The product must stay understandable: the agent works for the network, while you keep your machine, your keys, and your control.

local.title: No direct access to your models
local.text: Local AI stays behind the agent. It is not exposed as a public service anyone can call.

keys.title: Your keys stay with you
keys.text: Node identity is generated locally. The network does not ask you to hand over private keys.

control.title: You choose the rhythm
control.text: Your machine should not get in the way of your sessions. You decide when it can contribute.

stop.title: You can stop
stop.text: When you need the full PC power, you can stop or pause contribution.

final.title: Estimate first. Download next.
final.text: The best path is simple: check your machine's potential, then install the agent if the result is worth it for your usage.

particuliers.subtitle: For contributors with GPUs/CPUs who want quick onboarding and transparent revenue tracking.

valuePoints.1: Run the agent locally and expose eligible LocalAI models.
valuePoints.2: Get token-based accounting aligned with delivered inference.
valuePoints.3: Contribute to fallback pools when enterprise nodes are unavailable.
valuePoints.4: Prepare for future P2P model distribution to reduce egress costs.

seo.title: Infera - Offer for enterprises
seo.description: Sovereign OpenAI-compatible AI API, simple integration, fallback, carbon reporting, and territorial networks for enterprises.

entreprises.title: Enterprise Offer
entreprises.heroTitle: Sovereign AI that integrates without rebuilding your applications.
entreprises.heroSubtitle: Infera exposes an OpenAI-compatible API and routes inference to distributed compute pools according to your cost, residency, trust, availability, and energy-efficiency policies.
entreprises.accountCtaDescription: Create your account and quickly validate performance, costs, and compatibility for your AI workflows.

sovereignty.title: Operational sovereignty
sovereignty.text: Open-weight models, routing policies, and control over infrastructure tiers.

integration.title: Configuration-led migration
integration.text: Same OpenAI SDK logic: base URL, API key, models, and quotas.

control.title: Enterprise governance
control.text: Cost, trust, residency, carbon, and fallback become explicit parameters.

console.title: Drop-in OpenAI-compatible
console.subtitle: A progressive switch without heavy product rewrites.

items.dropin: Replace the inference target in application configuration.
items.routing: Filter by trust level: Public, Trusted, HDS/SecNumCloud when the scope is eligible.
items.reporting: Track tokens, costs, availability, and energy events from the backoffice.

entreprises.proofTitle: The challenge is no longer only buying tokens. It is governing where, how, and at what cost AI runs.
entreprises.proofSubtitle: Enterprises want the simplicity of centralized APIs with stronger control over vendor dependency, operational resilience, audit data, and environmental footprint.

sovereign.title: Use cost-efficient sovereign AI
sovereign.description: We route each workload to relevant open-weight models without creating structural dependency on a single proprietary API.

points.1: Business-fit selection for RAG, extraction, classification, internal support, and document batch.
points.2: Routing by cost, latency, residency, and trust level.
points.3: Pragmatic positioning: frontier models can remain elsewhere while Infera covers open-weight-friendly workloads.

nocode.title: Simple, near no-code integration
nocode.description: Teams update the inference layer configuration instead of rebuilding application flows.

points.1: OpenAI-compatible standard to preserve SDKs, prompts, and existing chains.
points.2: Fast pilots on a limited business scope before scaling.
points.3: API keys, quotas, and reporting built for product, IT, and finance teams.

hardware.title: Monetize unused GPUs and CPUs
hardware.description: Install the Infera agent on your infrastructure to turn dormant capacity into governed resources.

points.1: Rent surplus capacity within time windows and policies defined by your organization.
points.2: Reduce new capacity purchases when internal hardware is underused.
points.3: Keep role separation: private keys are generated locally on the node.

carbon.title: AI carbon accounting compatible with CSRD Scope 3
carbon.description: Infera turns the footprint of your AI usage into steering data for sustainability, procurement, finance, and CSRD Scope 3 paths.

points.1: Reading by request, model, pool, period, and confidence level when telemetry is available.
points.2: Scope 3-compatible attribution to connect digital services, AI usage, and supplier reporting.
points.3: Exports designed to connect footprint, cost, routing, and optimization decisions.

security.title: Secure critical operations
security.description: The on-premise Infera network version keeps a controlled fallback plan for sensitive AI operations.

points.1: Fallback to the Infera grid when internal nodes are saturated, under maintenance, or unavailable.
points.2: mTLS, pull/long-polling, and local agents reduce exposed surface.
points.3: Distinct packaging for Trusted, HDS, or SecNumCloud needs according to the actually qualified scope.

territory.title: Build territorial networks
territory.description: Bring together companies, public actors, schools, healthcare entities, or industrial groups to mutualize local AI capacity.

points.2: Local governance of capacity, routing rules, and priorities.
points.3: Collective resilience for sensitive workloads that should not depend on a single central point.

governance.title: Secure AI operations while mutualizing available resources.
governance.subtitle: Infera is sold as a control plane: decide which workloads run where, with which trust level, fallback, audit evidence, and carbon reading.

policies.title: Execution policies
policies.text: Route by business criticality, residency, cost, model, availability, and trust tier without changing integrations.

security.title: Secured operations
security.text: Isolate experimentation, production, and sensitive perimeters with mTLS, pull-based agents, and clear grid separation.

mutualization.title: Mutualized resources
mutualization.text: Reuse internal GPUs, trusted partners, or territorial capacity before systematically buying new capacity.

carbon.title: Usable carbon evidence
carbon.text: Connect tokens, energy, pools, and costs to steer sustainability commitments and CSRD Scope 3-compatible obligations.

integration.title: Start with one workload, not a global migration.
integration.subtitle: The right enterprise path proves compatibility on a controlled flow, then expands routing policies, quotas, and guarantees.

scope.title: Scope the first flow
scope.text: Identify an open-weight-friendly use case: internal RAG, document extraction, classification, support, or batch.

switch.title: Switch configuration
switch.text: Update base URL, API key, model, and trust parameters without rebuilding applications.

policy.title: Enable policies
policy.text: Choose cost, residency, trust tier, fallback, and reporting constraints according to flow criticality.

scale.title: Industrialize
scale.text: Connect metrics, quotas, keys, audits, and capacity rules to move from pilot to run.

operations.title: Three ways to de-risk AI operations.
operations.subtitle: Infera is not a raw GPU marketplace: it is a control layer for orchestrating distributed pools with enterprise rules.

fallback.title: Infera Grid fallback
fallback.subtitle: Service continuity
fallback.text: Use the Infera grid as fallback capacity when internal resources are saturated, under maintenance, or unavailable.

onpremise.title: On-premise network
onpremise.subtitle: Maximum control
onpremise.text: Deploy router, agents, and policies within your perimeter to use GPUs/CPUs while retaining operational governance.

federation.title: Territorial federation
federation.subtitle: Local mutualization
federation.text: Create a shared network between local actors to distribute capacity, priorities, and trust guarantees.

guardrails.title: What we clearly promise, and what we frame.
guardrails.subtitle: A credible enterprise page must help IT, business, procurement, and compliance teams. Strong promises must remain verifiable.

frontier.title: Infera does not replace every frontier model
frontier.text: Value is highest for workloads suited to open-weight models: document automation, RAG, extraction, classification, internal copilots, and batch. Frontier needs can remain routed elsewhere.

compliance.title: CSRD, HDS, and SecNumCloud require precise scope
compliance.text: Infera prepares steering data and target architectures. Final compliance depends on deployed scope, certified partners, contracts, and actual operations.

sensitive.title: Sensitive data does not go to any grid
sensitive.text: Public, Trusted, and HDS/SecNumCloud tiers must remain separate. Policy routing avoids mixing low-risk tests with regulated operations.

final.title: Let's audit your AI flows in 30 minutes.
final.text: We identify compatible use cases, quick wins, security constraints, and the right scenario: managed API, fallback, on-premise, or territorial network.

seo.title: Infera - Institutions and territories offer
seo.description: Federate territorial AI capacity with governance, auditability, and carbon accounting compatible with CSRD Scope 3.

hero.badge: For territories, administrations, and public-interest organizations
hero.title: Federate AI compute capacity across your territory.
hero.subtitle: Infera helps local governments, administrations, NGOs, and coalitions organize distributed, governed, and resilient inference capacity. The goal is not to move every AI workload onto one grid, but to make execution location, trust levels, costs, fallback paths, audit evidence, and CSRD Scope 3-compatible carbon accounting explicit.

sovereignty.title: Operational sovereignty
sovereignty.text: Choose perimeters, trust tiers, and execution policies instead of absorbing centralized dependency by default.

resilience.title: Multi-actor resilience
resilience.text: Pool local resources to keep useful AI workloads running when primary capacity is saturated or unavailable.

sobriety.title: Measured sobriety
sobriety.text: Prioritize existing capacity and structure usage, energy, and cost signals so decisions are evidence-based.

items.policies: Policy routing: cost, trust, residency, availability.
items.pull: Pull-based agents: no direct exposure of local capacity.
items.audit: Traceable usage, events, tokens, and energy signals.

why.title: The public-sector issue is not only accessing AI. It is controlling the conditions under which AI runs.
why.subtitle: Public-interest organizations must combine service continuity, budget control, strategic autonomy, regulatory constraints, and environmental responsibility. An Infera federation provides a framework to arbitrate these dimensions without claiming that one model or provider can serve every use case.

federate.title: Federate trusted actors
federate.text: Bring together local governments, public institutions, associations, schools, labs, hospitals, or sovereign partners around explicit access and priority rules.

derisk.title: Derisk sensitive AI operations
derisk.text: Separate experimentation, production, and regulated perimeters with trust tiers, mTLS certificates, and governance that decision-makers can understand.

costs.title: Steer costs before promising savings
costs.text: Compare local capacity, external fallback, latency, availability, and criticality to reduce reflexive purchases of centralized capacity.

ecology.title: Serve more sovereign and ecological AI
ecology.text: Value existing resources first and structure CSRD Scope 3-compatible carbon accounting instead of adding infrastructure without visibility into actual use.

scenarios.title: Four paths to turn a coalition into useful AI capacity.
scenarios.subtitle: The value comes from a shared operating framework: each actor keeps its role, while available capacity becomes steerable, measurable, and mobilizable through common policies.

territory.title: Territorial federation
territory.subtitle: Local governments, campuses, hospitals, associations
territory.text: Build a local pool where actors contribute capacity according to their constraints while sharing governance over priorities and trust levels.

continuity.title: AI continuity plan
continuity.subtitle: Resilience for digital public services
continuity.text: Prepare fallback routes for document, internal assistant, or batch workloads when primary resources no longer respond.

budget.title: Budget mutualization
budget.subtitle: Fewer isolated purchases, better steering
budget.text: Make dormant capacity and per-use costs visible to arbitrate between external purchases, local reuse, and staged scaling.

generalInterest.title: Public-interest AI
generalInterest.subtitle: NGOs, mediation, citizen support
generalInterest.text: Support socially useful workloads with appropriate open-weight models: document search, extraction, classification, summarization, and first-level assistance.

governance.title: Start small, but define from day one the rules that will let the network grow.
governance.subtitle: A credible federation does not start with a broad promise. It starts with mapping, a pilot perimeter, verifiable policies, and operating metrics.

map.title: Map resources and stakeholders
map.text: Identify actors, available machines, network constraints, eligible workloads, and legal or operational responsibilities.

policies.title: Define trust policies
policies.text: Separate Public, Trusted, and regulated perimeters. Specify residency, fallback, audit, quotas, model licenses, and prioritization rules.

pilot.title: Deploy one useful flow
pilot.text: Choose a use case compatible with open-weight models: document search, internal support, extraction, classification, or batch processing.

measure.title: Measure before expanding
measure.text: Track availability, tokens, cost, incidents, perceived quality, and CSRD Scope 3-compatible energy/carbon signals to decide objectively whether to extend the network.

carbon.title: An AI federation must also produce usable carbon evidence.
carbon.subtitle: The point is not only to say that existing capacity is more sober. You need to explain which workloads consume, on which pools, with which confidence level, and how these signals feed Scope 3 reporting.

scope3.title: CSRD Scope 3-compatible
scope3.text: Outsourced and mutualized AI usage can be connected to digital-service procurement and supplier evidence.

allocation.title: Usage allocation
allocation.text: Reading by model, period, pool, and perimeter avoids mixing experimentation, production, and fallback.

procurement.title: Procurement decisions
procurement.text: Teams can arbitrate between local capacity, fallback, new purchases, and sobriety requirements on a traceable basis.

guardrails.title: Strong promises, clearly scoped.
guardrails.subtitle: Administrations and NGOs do not need magic rhetoric. They need a framework that is understandable, contractable, and auditable.

certification.title: HDS, SecNumCloud, and compliance are not declarations
certification.text: Infera prepares architecture and steering data. Final compliance depends on scope, certified partners, contracts, and real operating conditions.

sensitive.title: Sensitive data requires dedicated perimeters
sensitive.text: A territorial federation does not mean everything can run everywhere. Policies must prevent mixing experimentation, production, and regulated data.

savings.title: Savings must be proven use case by use case
savings.text: Cost reduction depends on models, availability, utilization rate, and latency requirements. Infera helps measure and arbitrate before promising.

ecology.title: Sobriety is measured, not declared
ecology.text: Using existing capacity is a positive signal, but impact depends on energy mix, hardware efficiency, routing quality, and the load actually avoided.

final.title: Let's scope your federation scenario in 30 minutes.
final.text: We identify the actors to federate, compatible workloads, security constraints, metrics to track, and the right first pilot to move forward without overpromising.

billing.price_disclaimer: Estimate before taxes and rounding, based on the model catalog and active coefficients.
billing.pricing_unavailable: Backend pricing coefficients are unavailable. Local fallback values are being used.

public.description: Public routing for non-sensitive workloads and minimal cost.

trusted.description: Attested nodes for enterprise workloads and more sensitive data.

hds.description: Health-data oriented perimeter with stronger compliance posture.

fallback.title: Safe fallback
fallback.description: Allows Infera-operated capacity when no eligible grid node is available. Route uplift {uplift}% · x{multiplier}.
fallback.enabled_hint: Applied only to requests routed through fallback capacity.

audit_trail.title: Audit trail
audit_trail.description: Evidentiary log of requests, routing decisions and billing events. {uplift}% uplift · x{multiplier}.
audit_trail.enabled_hint: Persisted in the key policy and copied into usage events.

governance.forced_off: Unavailable because the organization has forced this option off.

banned_words.title: Banned words detector
banned_words.description: Case-insensitive detector with accent and leetspeak normalization. Matches are searched as substrings.

pseudonymization.title: Pseudonymization
pseudonymization.description: Deterministically replaces detected emails, phone numbers and simple person names before routing.

carbon.title: Carbon accounting
carbon.description: Prepares usage-level carbon reporting, with no backend effect in this version.
carbon.tooltip: Disabled until backend carbon measurement is available.

native_provider.title: Migration routing
native_provider.description: Switch all traffic between the Infera grid and the customer's original OpenAI-compatible provider.

traffic_experiment.title: Canary / AB testing
traffic_experiment.description: Routes a controlled share of matching requests to the original provider. The multiplier applies to all key usage while enabled.

spend_limits.title: Spend limits
spend_limits.caption: Soft monthly and lifetime warnings. Requests are not blocked.
spend_limits.required: Set a monthly or lifetime amount to enable spend-limit alerts.

secret.title: Copy key now

hardware.catalog_subtitle: Filter the catalog using capabilities detected in your browser.
hardware.partial_confidence: WebGPU not available: results use WebGL/browser heuristics with partial confidence.

runtime.cpu_fallback_opt_in: GPU profile by default. CPU fallback is degraded and only advertised when the node explicitly enables it.
runtime.cpu_gpu_available: Eligible on CPU and GPU slots.

detail.hardware_gpu_summary: Requires a GPU profile with at least {vram} GB VRAM and {ram} GB RAM.
detail.hardware_cpu_summary: CPU-capable profile requiring at least {cores} cores and {ram} GB RAM.

seo.title: Infera - Download an agent node
seo.description: Download Public, native, and Trusted Tier Infera agent artifacts for node operators.

consumer.label: Individual
consumer.title: Turn your own machine into an Infera node
consumer.subtitle: Start with the simplest distribution for a personal machine, lab box, or side GPU. We prioritize a fast first install and clear explanations over infrastructure jargon.
consumer.recommendedSubtitle: Best fit for a first node on this device.

1.title: Start fast
1.body: The light bundle is the quickest path to your first online node.

2.title: Works on your current OS
2.body: We surface installers and bundles that match the system we detected.

3.title: Learn as you go
3.body: Every option explains what is included and what still downloads later.

enterprise.label: Enterprise
enterprise.title: Choose the rollout path that matches production controls
enterprise.subtitle: For teams, the page now prioritizes trust, auditability, and deployment predictability. Trusted Tier moves to the front, while Public and native paths remain documented for pilots and labs.

1.title: Trust first
1.body: Trusted Tier is promoted ahead of convenience-oriented packaging.

2.title: Evidence visible
2.body: Checksum, signatures, and attestation artifacts stay explicit.

3.title: Economics explained
3.body: The simulator shows the Trusted multiplier instead of hiding it in policy.

apple.title: macOS is reserved for the Trusted experience
apple.subtitle: Apple visitors are directed to the Trusted story only. No Public or native alternatives are shown here because the macOS proposition is intentionally positioned as a trusted-capacity lane.
apple.recommendedSubtitle: macOS is treated as a Trusted-only lane on this page.

1.title: Trusted by design
1.body: Apple hardware is framed as premium, controlled capacity.

2.title: Benchmark-friendly
2.body: The integrated browser benchmark helps users understand local capability before they install.

3.title: Multiplier shown
3.body: Trusted uplift is visible directly in the revenue estimate.

release.hybridSource: This page mixes backend metadata with the live static release tree so published downloads stay visible even when the release index is incomplete.

states.error: The latest release could not be loaded. Try again later or use the documented static mirror.
states.noRecommended: No compatible artifact is available for this device in the latest release.

artifacts.readyNote: Direct download available from the static release mirror.
artifacts.pendingNote: This artifact is part of the intended matrix but is not published on the mirror yet.

apple.title: Lucky Apple guys, you are trusted tier provider!
apple.body: On macOS we deliberately hide Public and native alternatives. This page treats Apple hardware as a Trusted-capacity story, with the trust note and multiplier explained upfront.

light.title: Light bundle
light.subtitle: Smallest starting point
light.body: Downloads runtime pieces later. Best when you want the fastest first install and do not need offline payloads.

images.title: Images bundle
images.subtitle: Runtime images included
images.body: Useful when you want the container images ready locally, while model downloads can still happen later.

embedded.title: Embedded bundle
embedded.subtitle: Heavier but more complete
embedded.body: Carries more payload upfront for operators who want fewer downloads during first startup.

publicTier.title: Public Tier
publicTier.description: Public Tier is the fastest way to bring community capacity online. It keeps the Docker-first runtime, local key generation, and pull-based agent behavior intact.
publicTier.enterpriseDescription: Public Tier remains useful for pilots, internal labs, and staged rollouts, but it is intentionally presented behind Trusted for enterprise visitors.
publicTier.explainerBody: These bundles are not a dedicated operating system. They package the agent runtime in different completeness levels and keep the router-to-agent trust model intact.
publicTier.empty: No Public Tier bundle is available in the latest release.

steps.1: Download the light or images bundle for your host.
steps.2: Start the node with the public profile and keep LocalAI behind the sidecar.
steps.3: Generate node keys locally during enrollment.
steps.4: Poll the Router for tasks once the node is accepted.

native.title: Native installers
native.description: Native wrappers improve installation ergonomics while preserving the validated Docker-first runtime underneath.
native.enterpriseDescription: Native packages are useful when teams want a friendlier host install, while still accepting the same Docker-first runtime model below.
native.runtimeNote: Native installers still depend on the existing agent image bundle and host Docker runtime unless the artifact explicitly says otherwise.
native.explainerTitle: Native does not mean separate platform logic
native.explainerBody: The installer feels more familiar, but the operational model stays aligned with the same release lane and runtime guardrails.

trustedTier.title: Trusted Tier
trustedTier.description: Trusted Tier artifacts are built for hardened operations: immutable boot image, attested OS profile, release signature, and fail-closed trust policy.
trustedTier.enterpriseDescription: Trusted Tier is the production-grade lane for operators who care about verification, policy, and predictable capacity qualification.
trustedTier.appleDescription: For macOS visitors, Trusted is not shown as a side option but as the only narrative. Apple hardware is framed as premium capacity for the trusted lane.
trustedTier.explainerBody: Trusted packages are about a stronger chain of custody. The ISO represents a dedicated operating system image, while the evidence files let operators verify what they are about to run.
trustedTier.isoNote: Trusted ISO is presented here as the dedicated OS path. It is the only lane intended to render a node eligible for the Trusted tier once verification and onboarding policy are satisfied.

steps.1: Download the Trusted image and its verification artifacts.
steps.2: Verify the checksum, signature, and attested OS profile before boot.
steps.3: Boot the controlled host and complete node enrollment.
steps.4: Run workloads only after Router trust policy accepts the attested profile.

points.1: Dedicated OS image to boot on bare-metal or controlled VMs.
points.2: Hardened trust chain for sensitive environments.
points.3: Specific onboarding workflow for SLA-operated nodes.

macos.name: macOS
macos.description: Trusted-only presentation for Apple Silicon oriented operators.

linux.name: Linux
linux.description: Recommended path for community-grade production deployments with Docker Compose.

windows.name: Windows
windows.description: Guided setup with Docker Desktop and bootstrap scripts.

releaseNotes.title: Release notes
releaseNotes.empty: No changelog is attached to this release yet.

simulator.title: Capacity and revenue simulator
simulator.subtitle: Estimate what this machine can contribute, then compare the Public baseline with the Trusted uplift.
simulator.previewSubtitle: A quick read based on detected hardware and model catalog prices.
simulator.publicModeDescription: An everyday companion that starts when your computer is on.
simulator.trustedModeDescription: A dedicated OS you boot when you are not using your computer. The multiplier comes from the active pricing policy.
simulator.appleModeDescription: On Apple, the experience stays a Trusted companion adapted to the macOS flow.
simulator.multiplierDetail: {base} baseline becomes {trusted} with the Trusted coefficient.
simulator.trustedReadySubtitle: The detected hardware looks compatible with a stronger capacity story.
simulator.trustedReadyBody: This does not replace formal verification, but it indicates the machine can credibly be positioned for the Trusted lane once policy and release evidence requirements are met.
simulator.trustedGapSubtitle: This machine reads better as Public or native for now.
simulator.trustedGapBody: You can still install and operate the node, but the benchmark does not make a strong trusted-capacity case yet.
simulator.appleTrustedNote: Apple visitors are treated as a Trusted narrative by default, so the uplift is shown as the main commercial framing.
simulator.multiplierExplainBody: The Trusted estimate is the same baseline capacity multiplied by x{multiplier}. We show the multiplication explicitly so the user sees where the uplift comes from.
simulator.benchmarkExplainBody: It combines WebGL/WebGPU signals, runtime inference, and catalog heuristics to build a practical first estimate before installation.
simulator.modelsSubtitle: {count} models match the detected hardware profile ({runtime}).
simulator.modelsEmpty: No paid catalog model matches the detected hardware yet.
simulator.partialConfidence: WebGPU not available: results rely on browser heuristics and should be treated as directional.
simulator.disclaimer: This estimate is indicative and depends on model demand, routing policy, availability, and accepted tier.

matrix.title: Tier matrix
matrix.subtitle: Quick comparison of what each lane really means before you download.
matrix.appleSubtitle: On macOS we keep the matrix focused on the Trusted story only.

public.internet: Usually downloads more at first run
public.os: No, runs on your current host

native.internet: Still relies on the same runtime lane underneath
native.os: No, wraps the current host OS

trusted.internet: Can support more controlled rollout logic
trusted.eligible: Only lane meant for Trusted-tier eligibility

apiSurface.title: Release API surface
apiSurface.description: The frontend now consumes the download release contract exposed by the backend.

gaps.1: The backend release contract is not yet sufficient on its own to present every published distribution artifact.
gaps.2: Checksum and signature artifacts should remain visible whenever the Trusted lane is published.
gaps.3: Compatibility policy between agent, router, host OS, and tier should become explicit in the API.
gaps.4: The static release tree and the release index need to stay consistent to avoid UX drift.

tier_assignment.title: This node is applying for the {tier} grid

identity_status.pending: Identity validation is still completed from your account space for trusted-tier routing.

actions.helper: You can always deny the challenge. Approval stays locked until the checklist is complete.

registration.waiting: Waiting for heartbeat, certificate, and runtime signals...
registration.success_title: Node validated on the Infera grid
registration.success_subtitle: All checks are green. You can now manage this node from your space.

disable.title: Disable organization
disable.body: This organization will stop being available for active administration workflows. Existing audit history remains visible.

not_found.description: This page is not available in the selected locale or default locale.

forbidden.title: Restricted content
forbidden.description: This page requires an admin session.

playground.description: Test Infera router OpenAI-compatible APIs with your own billed API keys.

cta.trial_title: First time? €10 free to test the platform
cta.trial_caption: Create an account, generate a test key and run live inference in a few minutes.

hints.billing: Requests are sent to Infera router using the selected API key and are billed through the standard pipeline.
hints.openapi: Browse the public OpenAPI contract used for client SDK generation.
hints.no_result: Run a request to see the response here.
hints.unauthenticated: Sign in to use your billed API keys and access live inference logs.
hints.completions_output: Plain-text output is rendered first, with raw payload still available.
hints.images_output: Rendered images stay inline so users can inspect the result immediately.
hints.speech_output: Speech responses are converted to a previewable audio stream in the browser.
hints.microphone_capture: Capture a short audio clip directly in the browser, then submit it as a file.
hints.transcription_output: Transcript text is surfaced first, with the underlying response preserved below.
hints.moderation_output: Severity bars make category scores readable without opening raw JSON.
hints.files_output: Upload returns the created file metadata that downstream APIs will reference.

errors.configuration_required: Select a valid API key and a model before running a request.
errors.playground_timeout: Request timed out while waiting for the router.
errors.playground_payload_too_large: Payload is too large for playground relay limits.
errors.playground_rate_limited: Rate limit reached. Please retry later.
errors.playground_upstream_unavailable: Router is unavailable. Please retry shortly.
errors.playground_router_timeout: Playground timed out before the router returned a response.
errors.playground_router_unreachable: Playground cannot reach the router. Check the local router process and URL.
errors.playground_router_transport_error: Playground lost the router connection before a response was returned.
errors.agent_timeout: The agent timed out processing your request. The model may still be loading - please retry.
errors.agent_failure: The agent failed to process the request. Please retry.
errors.invalid_request_error: The selected model does not support this endpoint. Choose a compatible model or switch playground tab.
errors.audit_trail_disabled: Audit trail is not enabled for this API key.
errors.audio_file_required: Select or record an audio file before running a transcription.
errors.file_required: Select a file before uploading it.
errors.microphone_unavailable: Microphone recording is not available in this browser.

identity.mock_notice: Mocked status for current MVP version.

nodes.title: My agents
nodes.subtitle: Provider agents linked to your account

keys.title: My API keys
keys.subtitle: Usage and cost by API key

nodes.title: No agents yet
nodes.subtitle: Install an agent to get started
nodes.description: Turn your hardware into revenue and track earnings once an agent is connected.

keys.title: No API keys yet
keys.subtitle: Create a key to call the API
keys.description: Create per-environment keys for better security and traceability.

nodes.title: My nodes
nodes.subtitle: Follow enrolled hardware, runtime state and estimated provider revenue.

retire.title: Reset node enrollment
retire.subtitle: This revokes {nodeId}. The local agent must be started with a new enrollment token to join again.
retire.error: Unable to reset this node enrollment.

enrollment_token.title: Enrollment token
enrollment_token.subtitle: This token is short-lived and binds the next public node enrollment to your operator account.
enrollment_token.error: Unable to create an enrollment token.

keys.title: My API keys
keys.subtitle: Create, rotate, revoke and route API keys attached to your workspace.

billing.title: Billing & payment
billing.subtitle: Track balance, payment methods and invoice history.
billing.missing_surface: Production gap: payment method editing and invoice download actions need dedicated backend operations before this can be fully self-service.

payment_methods.title: Payment methods
payment_methods.empty_text: Add-card and default-payment controls are not exposed by the current API.

organisations.title: Organisations
organisations.subtitle: Review the current organisation and member access.
organisations.missing_surface: Production gap: invitations, role changes and organisation switching are partially available in APIs but need a dedicated user-safe workflow.

security.title: Security
security.subtitle: Review SSO claims and production security controls.

production.title: Production controls to expose
production.keys: API key rotation and revocation are available from the Keys page.
production.identity: Identity validation belongs in Personal informations.
production.sessions: Session inventory and forced logout are missing backend surfaces.

informations.title: Informations
informations.subtitle: Billing address, account type and preferred currency.

account.title: Account
account.currency_hint: ISO 4217 code (e.g. EUR, USD). Used in all monetary displays.

otp.title: Node enrollment token
otp.subtitle: Generate a short-lived token to authenticate your node's first enrollment.

hardware.title: Hardware identity
hardware.subtitle: This token will be bound to the machine below. It cannot be reused on different hardware.

otp.token_hint: This token is single-use and expires in {ttl} seconds.
otp.inject_hint: Run this command in your node terminal to inject the token automatically:
otp.auto_claim_waiting: Your node is waiting for this token and will claim it automatically. The command remains available as a fallback.
otp.sign_in_required: Sign in to generate a hardware-bound enrollment token.
otp.error: Unable to generate the enrollment token.
otp.error_disabled: Token generation is not configured on this server. Contact your administrator.
otp.error_auth: Session expired. Reload the page and sign in again.
otp.no_agent: No agent ID provided. Open this page from your node terminal.

claimed.title: See you in a moment.
claimed.subtitle: The node claimed its token. You can return to the terminal to finish enrollment.

admin_models.title: Model management
admin_models.description: Manage the model catalog synced from gallery referential, set tier policies, and configure per-node overrides.

catalog.empty: No models in catalog. Run a sync to load from gallery referential.

policy.title: Tier model policy
policy.select_models: Select desired models for this tier
policy.save_success: Policy saved. Agents will reconcile on next heartbeat.
policy.table_empty: No model selected for this tier.

overrides.empty: No overrides configured for this node.
overrides.select_node: Select a node to view and manage overrides.
